title: Calling podman run to load a kernel module via a systemd unit causes triggers EACCES(Permission denied) from SELinux.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

/kind bug

**Description**

Calling podman run to load a kernel module via a systemd unit causes triggers EACCES(Permission denied) from SELinux.

```
podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} \
    insmod /usr/lib/modules/${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}/extra/wireguard.ko
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a systemd unit file that uses podman to load a kernel module

2. Run the unit with SELinux set to enforcing

3. Think of dwalsh's feelings when you are tempted to turn off SELinux

4. Open libpod issue instead

**Describe the results you received:**

```
# ausearch -m avc -i
type=PROCTITLE msg=audit(05/18/2018 18:28:09.833:1983) : proctitle=insmod /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko 
type=SYSCALL msg=audit(05/18/2018 18:28:09.833:1983) : arch=x86_64 syscall=finit_module success=no exit=EACCES(Permission denied) a0=0x3 a1=0x55c85b1c03e6 a2=0x0 a3=0x3 items=0 ppid=10330 pid=10340 auid=unset uid=root gid=root euid=root suid=root fsuid=root egid=root sgid=root fsgid=root tty=(none) ses=unset comm=insmod exe=/usr/bin/kmod subj=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 key=(null) 
type=AVC msg=audit(05/18/2018 18:28:09.833:1983) : avc:  denied  { module_load } for  pid=10340 comm=insmod path=/usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko dev="dm-0" ino=68203196 scontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0 tclass=system permissive=0 
```

Kicking SELinux into permissive mode allows the module to be loaded via systemd.

**Describe the results you expected:**

No SELinux issues when trying to insert a kernel module on the main host via podman run when called from a systemd unit file.

Running this manually:

```
podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} \
    insmod /usr/lib/modules/${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}/extra/wireguard.ko
```

as the root user does not cause any SELinux issues.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.5.1
Go Version:    go1.10.1
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  MemFree: 219496448
  MemTotal: 1032495104
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: atomic
  kernel: 4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 73h 5m 52.35s (Approximately 3.04 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is running on a KVM based Fedora 28 Atomic Host Vagrant image.

comments.body:
I updated container-selinux today for these issues.

container-selinux-2.58-1.gitd213769.fc27
container-selinux-2.58-1.gitd213769.fc28

Please install these and try it again.  You might need to run restorecon -v /usr/bin/podman

Thanks @rhatdan for the help. I installed that RPM and rebooted and it shows it as installed:

[root@atomic ~]# rpm -qa |grep container-selinux
container-selinux-2.58-1.gitd213769.fc28.noarch

but I am seeing the same results:

```
May 18 21:36:03 atomic atomic-wireguard-module[4061]: insmod: ERROR: could not insert module /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko: Permission denied
```

```
----
type=PROCTITLE msg=audit(05/18/2018 21:36:03.546:750) : proctitle=insmod /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko 
type=SYSCALL msg=audit(05/18/2018 21:36:03.546:750) : arch=x86_64 syscall=finit_module success=no exit=EACCES(Permission denied) a0=0x3 a1=0x55b3e554e3e6 a2=0x0 a3=0x3 items=0 ppid=4132 pid=4142 auid=unset uid=root gid=root euid=root suid=root fsuid=root egid=root sgid=root fsgid=root tty=(none) ses=unset comm=insmod exe=/usr/bin/kmod subj=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 key=(null) 
type=AVC msg=audit(05/18/2018 21:36:03.546:750) : avc:  denied  { module_load } for  pid=4142 comm=insmod path=/usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko dev="dm-0" ino=68203196 scontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0 tclass=system permissive=0
```

Did you run restorecon on /usr/bin/podman?
restorecon -v /usr/bin/podman
ls -lZ /usr/bin/podman

Yeah, I did the restorecon:

```
[vagrant@atomic ~]$ restorecon -v /usr/bin/podman
[vagrant@atomic ~]$ ls -lZ /usr/bin/podman
-rwxr-xr-x. 3 root root system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 21694688 Jan  1  1970 /usr/bin/podman
```

Now make sure podman is not running.  Or is running with the container_runtime_t label.

ps -eZ | grep podman

If it is running with a different label the contiainer_runtime_t, kill it and restart the service, it should not run as container_runtime_t.

Weird, podman is still running with the `unconfined_service_t` label when being called by systemd. Instead of running `insmod /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko` I just called `sleep 60`:

```
[root@atomic ~]# ps -auxZ | grep podman
system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 root 9849 0.0  2.4 593988 24584 ?    Sl   16:37   0:00 podman run --name wireguard -e WIREGUARD_VERSION=0.0.20180513 -e WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64 --rm --privileged wireguard:0.0.20180513 sleep 60
system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 root 9912 0.0  0.1 85936 1860 ?      Ssl  16:37   0:00 /usr/libexec/podman/conmon -c 11d93ec01074285c57a07b9e4c2efdb20f7b9c6f79d2995445cf20cecd7a384e -u 11d93ec01074285c57a07b9e4c2efdb20f7b9c6f79d2995445cf20cecd7a384e -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/11d93ec01074285c57a07b9e4c2efdb20f7b9c6f79d2995445cf20cecd7a384e/userdata -p /var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/11d93ec01074285c57a07b9e4c2efdb20f7b9c6f79d2995445cf20cecd7a384e/userdata/pidfile -l /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/11d93ec01074285c57a07b9e4c2efdb20f7b9c6f79d2995445cf20cecd7a384e/userdata/ctr.log --exit-dir /var/run/libpod/exits --socket-dir-path /var/run/libpod/socket```
```

I am running Atomic in `ostree admin unlock --hotfix` right now. Would that impact things here?

I am not sure @cgwalters any ideas?  

@jdoss What does the unit file you are executing to run podman look like?

@rhatdan A pretty standard oneshot unit file:

```
[root@atomic ~]# cat /etc/systemd/system/atomic-wireguard.service 
[Unit]
Description=Atomic WireGuard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
EnvironmentFile=/etc/sysconfig/atomic-wireguard
TimeoutStartSec=30m
RemainAfterExit=true
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/atomic-wireguard-module build
ExecStart=/usr/bin/atomic-wireguard-module load
ExecStop=/usr/bin/atomic-wireguard-module unload

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

Here is the full bash script that is being called:

```
#!/bin/bash

set -e

WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=$(uname -r)

build_wireguard()
{

  if [[ "$(podman images -n |grep wireguard|awk '{print $2}')" =~ (^|[[:space:]])${WIREGUARD_VERSION}($|[[:space:]]) ]]; then

    echo "The WireGuard ${WIREGUARD_VERSION} kernel module container is already built."

    else

    echo "The WireGuard ${WIREGUARD_VERSION} kernel module container is not built."
    echo "Building ${WIREGUARD_VERSION} kernel module for ${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION} ..."

    podman build --build-arg WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION} --build-arg WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION} -t wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} -f /etc/wireguard/Dockerfile
  fi

  if [ "${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" != "$(podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} modinfo /usr/lib/modules/${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}/extra/wireguard.ko \
    |grep '^version'|awk '{print $2}')" ]; then

    echo "The WireGuard kernel module for ${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION} is not the correct version."
    echo "Building ${WIREGUARD_VERSION} kernel module for ${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION} ..."

    podman build --build-arg WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION} --build-arg WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION} -t wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} -f /etc/wireguard/Dockerfile
  fi

  echo "Cleaning up old WireGuard kernel module container builds..."

  podman rmi $(podman images -f "dangling=true" -q) &> /dev/null || true

  for OLD_IMAGES in $(podman images -n|grep wireguard|grep -v ${WIREGUARD_VERSION}|awk '{print $3}'); do
    podman rmi ${OLD_IMAGES} &> /dev/null || true
  done

}

unload_wireguard()
{
  echo "Unloading WireGuard..."
  if lsmod |grep "wireguard" &> /dev/null; then
    rmmod wireguard
  fi

  if lsmod | grep "udp_tunnel" &> /dev/null; then
    rmmod udp_tunnel
  fi

  if lsmod | grep "ip6_udp_tunnel" &> /dev/null; then
    rmmod ip6_udp_tunnel
  fi
}

load_wireguard()
{
  echo "Loading WireGuard..."
  if ! lsmod | grep "udp_tunnel" &> /dev/null; then
    podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} modprobe udp_tunnel
  fi

  if ! lsmod | grep "ip6_udp_tunnel" &> /dev/null; then
    podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} modprobe ip6_udp_tunnel
  fi

  if ! lsmod |grep "wireguard" &> /dev/null; then
    podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} sleep 60
#    insmod /usr/lib/modules/${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}/extra/wireguard.ko
  fi
}

reload_wireguard()
{
  echo "Reloading WireGuard..."
  if lsmod |grep "wireguard" &> /dev/null; then
    rmmod wireguard
  fi

  if ! lsmod |grep "wireguard" &> /dev/null; then
    podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} \
    insmod /usr/lib/modules/${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}/extra/wireguard.ko
  fi
}

case "$1" in
        build)
          build_wireguard
        ;;

        load)
          load_wireguard
        ;;

        unload)
          unload_wireguard
        ;;

        reload)
          reload_wireguard
        ;;

        *)
          echo $"Unknown command. Exiting."
          echo "Usage:"
          echo ""
          echo "build       Build wireguard kernel module container"
          echo "load        Load wireguard kernel module"
          echo "unload      Unload wireguard kernel module"
          echo "reload      Reload wireguard kernel module"
          exit 1
esac
```

Well this is not just launching podman from the unit file, you are launching a script first and systemd transitions from init_t to unconfined_service_t when launching a shell_exec_t (/bin/sh) or a bin_t, Then when unconfined_service_t launches podman it does not transition.

I have to add a transition from unconfined_service_t to container_runtime_t over container_runtime_exec_t.
Ooof. Sorry for not being clear @rhatdan. Thanks for your help.

@jdoss could you try the container-selinux in updates testing 2.60 and update karma here.

https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2018-b0ea7fc23c

@rhatdan systemd is able to call:

```
podman build --build-arg WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION} --build-arg WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION} -t wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} -f /etc/wireguard/Dockerfile
```

now from within the bash script, but when the bash script calls:

```
    podman run --name wireguard -e "WIREGUARD_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_VERSION}" \
    -e "WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION=${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}" --rm --privileged \
    wireguard:${WIREGUARD_VERSION} \
    insmod /usr/lib/modules/${WIREGUARD_KERNEL_VERSION}/extra/wireguard.ko
```

to insmod the kernel module that was built within the container, it still hits SELinux issues. 

```
May 21 18:55:47 atomic atomic-wireguard-module[16377]: insmod: ERROR: could not insert module /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko: Permission denied
```

`#ausearch -m avc` -i shows:
 
```
----
type=PROCTITLE msg=audit(05/21/2018 18:55:47.539:684) : proctitle=insmod /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko 
type=SYSCALL msg=audit(05/21/2018 18:55:47.539:684) : arch=x86_64 syscall=finit_module success=no exit=EACCES(Permission denied) a0=0x3 a1=0x5629b38df3e6 a2=0x0 a3=0x3 items=0 ppid=16728 pid=16738 auid=unset uid=root gid=root euid=root suid=root fsuid=root egid=root sgid=root fsgid=root tty=(none) ses=unset comm=insmod exe=/usr/bin/kmod subj=system_u:system_r:spc_t:s0 key=(null) 
type=AVC msg=audit(05/21/2018 18:55:47.539:684) : avc:  denied  { module_load } for  pid=16738 comm=insmod path=/usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko dev="dm-0" ino=102940038 scontext=system_u:system_r:spc_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0 tclass=system permissive=0
```

Which is a different message than the original one which was `comm=insmod exe=/usr/bin/kmod subj=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 key=(null)` vs `comm=insmod exe=/usr/bin/kmod subj=system_u:system_r:spc_t:s0 key=(null) ` now.

```
# ausearch -m avc -i
type=PROCTITLE msg=audit(05/18/2018 18:28:09.833:1983) : proctitle=insmod /usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko 
type=SYSCALL msg=audit(05/18/2018 18:28:09.833:1983) : arch=x86_64 syscall=finit_module success=no exit=EACCES(Permission denied) a0=0x3 a1=0x55c85b1c03e6 a2=0x0 a3=0x3 items=0 ppid=10330 pid=10340 auid=unset uid=root gid=root euid=root suid=root fsuid=root egid=root sgid=root fsgid=root tty=(none) ses=unset comm=insmod exe=/usr/bin/kmod subj=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 key=(null) 
type=AVC msg=audit(05/18/2018 18:28:09.833:1983) : avc:  denied  { module_load } for  pid=10340 comm=insmod path=/usr/lib/modules/4.16.7-300.fc28.x86_64/extra/wireguard.ko dev="dm-0" ino=68203196 scontext=system_u:system_r:unconfined_service_t:s0 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0 tclass=system permissive=0

Unbelievable.  I have built more container-selinux packages in the last couple of days.  And hadn't touched in a month or two.

Fixed in container-selinux-2.61-1.git9b55129

@rhatdan Victory!!

```
[root@atomic ~]# systemctl start atomic-wireguard
[root@atomic ~]# getenforce 
Enforcing
[root@atomic ~]# rpm -qa |grep container-selinux
container-selinux-2.61-1.git9b55129.fc28.noarch
[root@atomic ~]# systemctl status atomic-wireguard
● atomic-wireguard.service - Atomic WireGuard

  Process: 2742 ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/atomic-wireguard-module build (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
 Main PID: 2998 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)

May 21 22:20:50 atomic conmon[3205]: conmon f4c548e6f59c54d54bac <ninfo>: container 3215 exited with status 0

May 21 22:20:50 atomic conmon[3346]: conmon 7a4be7f16f2160b6316e <ninfo>: ctl fifo path: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/7a4be7f16f2160b6316e666fc76c5fbfcf80dde5e4da35ee70ee3a38b5762d95/userdata/ctl

I can add some karma once you get it up on bodhi. Thanks a ton again for your help. I hope to bring a work around for kernel module support in Atomic with these efforts.
